sections.content: Let me check the current state after SPACE. I notice the image shows the charcoal at (28,16) now has TWO blue squares inside instead of one. This is interesting - SPACE might have duplicated the blue inside the charcoal.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] not in ['w', 'g']:
        print(f"color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something important:
- G object at (28,20)-(31,23) - 4x4 with blue inside
- White strip at (32,20)-(32,23) - 1x4 (shed from 5x4)
- Player at (33,20)-(35,23) - 3x4
So SPACE caused the white strip to shed from 5x4 to 1x4 and created a new G object at (28,20)-(31,23). The G object is to the left of the blue-gray rectangle.
Now I have:
- Charcoal at (24,44)-(27,47) - 4x4
- Charcoal at (28,16)-(31,19) - 4x4 (with 2 blue squares inside now!)
- G at (28,20)-(31,23) - 4x4
- Blue-gray at (28,28)-(31,39) - 4x12
- G from earlier (somewhere)
Wait, the charcoal at (28,16) now has 2 blue squares inside (children [6] and [7]... no, [6] is the blue at (29,17) and [7] is the blue at (29,21) which is inside the G).
Actually, looking more carefully:
- Charcoal at (28,16)-(31,19) has child [6] which is blue at (29,17)-(30,18)
- G at (28,20)-(31,23) has child [7] which is blue at (29,21)-(30,22)
So the G object has a blue square inside, just like the charcoals.
Now I have a 1x4 white strip at (32,20)-(32,23), directly below the G at (28,20)-(31,23). Let me try UP to see what happens.
Actually, let me think about the pattern:
1. Initial: 4x4 charcoal at (36,32) with 1x4 white strip below → UP → converts to G
2. Now: 4x4 G at (28,20) with 1x4 white strip below → UP → ???
Maybe UP on G does something different. Or maybe I need to position the white strip below a charcoal to convert it.
The charcoal at (28,16)-(31,19) is at cols 16-19. The white strip is at cols 20-23. I need to move LEFT to get below the charcoal.
Let me try LEFT to move the white strip to cols 16-19, then UP to convert the charcoal to G.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White strip at (32,20)-(32,23) - 1x4
# Charcoal at (28,16)-(31,19) - 4x4, to the left
# Move LEFT to align with charcoal, then UP to convert
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
